verses.text: At dawn the scribes bring a woman taken in adultery, set in the midst. A trap laid. They use her sin to ensnare Christ.
At dawn the scribes bring a woman taken in adultery, set in the midst. A trap laid. They use her sin to ensnare Christ.
At dawn the scribes bring a woman taken in adultery, set in the midst. A trap laid. They use her sin to ensnare Christ.
Moses bade such be stoned; what sayest thou? tempting Him; Jesus writes on the ground. Silent wisdom. He will not be caught by their craft.
Moses bade such be stoned; what sayest thou? tempting Him; Jesus writes on the ground. Silent wisdom. He will not be caught by their craft.
Moses bade such be stoned; what sayest thou? tempting Him; Jesus writes on the ground. Silent wisdom. He will not be caught by their craft.
'He that is without sin among you, let him first cast a stone'; convicted, they go out one by one. Conscience struck. None dares condemn who is himself guilty.
'He that is without sin among you, let him first cast a stone'; convicted, they go out one by one. Conscience struck. None dares condemn who is himself guilty.
'He that is without sin among you, let him first cast a stone'; convicted, they go out one by one. Conscience struck. None dares condemn who is himself guilty.
'Hath no man condemned thee? ... Neither do I condemn thee: go, and sin no more.' Mercy and charge. He pardons and calls to a changed life.
'Hath no man condemned thee? ... Neither do I condemn thee: go, and sin no more.' Mercy and charge. He pardons and calls to a changed life.
'I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall... have the light of life.' Light to follow. Christ leads His own out of darkness.
'I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall... have the light of life.' Light to follow. Christ leads His own out of darkness.
'I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall... have the light of life.' Light to follow. Christ leads His own out of darkness.
I judge no man now; if I judge, it is true, for the Father is with Me. Two witnesses. The Father and the Son together attest the truth.
I judge no man now; if I judge, it is true, for the Father is with Me. Two witnesses. The Father and the Son together attest the truth.
I judge no man now; if I judge, it is true, for the Father is with Me. Two witnesses. The Father and the Son together attest the truth.
I judge no man now; if I judge, it is true, for the Father is with Me. Two witnesses. The Father and the Son together attest the truth.
Where is thy Father? 'ye neither know me, nor my Father'; none laid hands on Him, His hour not come. Guarded hour. None can touch Him before God's time.
Where is thy Father? 'ye neither know me, nor my Father'; none laid hands on Him, His hour not come. Guarded hour. None can touch Him before God's time.
I go My way; 'if ye believe not that I am he, ye shall die in your sins.' Solemn warning. Unbelief leaves men to perish in their sin.
I go My way; 'if ye believe not that I am he, ye shall die in your sins.' Solemn warning. Unbelief leaves men to perish in their sin.
I go My way; 'if ye believe not that I am he, ye shall die in your sins.' Solemn warning. Unbelief leaves men to perish in their sin.
I go My way; 'if ye believe not that I am he, ye shall die in your sins.' Solemn warning. Unbelief leaves men to perish in their sin.
Who art thou? the same I said from the first; they understood not He spoke of the Father. Veiled to them. They cannot grasp His words of the Father.
Who art thou? the same I said from the first; they understood not He spoke of the Father. Veiled to them. They cannot grasp His words of the Father.
Who art thou? the same I said from the first; they understood not He spoke of the Father. Veiled to them. They cannot grasp His words of the Father.
'When ye have lifted up the Son of man, then shall ye know that I am he'; many believed. The cross reveals. His death would prove who He is.
'When ye have lifted up the Son of man, then shall ye know that I am he'; many believed. The cross reveals. His death would prove who He is.
'When ye have lifted up the Son of man, then shall ye know that I am he'; many believed. The cross reveals. His death would prove who He is.
'If ye continue in my word... ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.' True freedom. Abiding in His word brings liberty.
'If ye continue in my word... ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.' True freedom. Abiding in His word brings liberty.
'If ye continue in my word... ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.' True freedom. Abiding in His word brings liberty.
Whoso commits sin is sin's servant; 'if the Son... shall make you free, ye shall be free indeed.' Real liberty. Only Christ frees from the bondage of sin.
Whoso commits sin is sin's servant; 'if the Son... shall make you free, ye shall be free indeed.' Real liberty. Only Christ frees from the bondage of sin.
Whoso commits sin is sin's servant; 'if the Son... shall make you free, ye shall be free indeed.' Real liberty. Only Christ frees from the bondage of sin.
Ye seek to kill Me; ye do your father's deeds; 'Abraham did not so.' False sons. Their deeds deny their boasted descent.
Ye seek to kill Me; ye do your father's deeds; 'Abraham did not so.' False sons. Their deeds deny their boasted descent.
Ye seek to kill Me; ye do your father's deeds; 'Abraham did not so.' False sons. Their deeds deny their boasted descent.
Ye seek to kill Me; ye do your father's deeds; 'Abraham did not so.' False sons. Their deeds deny their boasted descent.
'Ye are of your father the devil'; he was 'a murderer from the beginning' and 'the father of it,' lying. Their true father. Hatred and lies betray whose children they are.
'Ye are of your father the devil'; he was 'a murderer from the beginning' and 'the father of it,' lying. Their true father. Hatred and lies betray whose children they are.
'Ye are of your father the devil'; he was 'a murderer from the beginning' and 'the father of it,' lying. Their true father. Hatred and lies betray whose children they are.
'Ye are of your father the devil'; he was 'a murderer from the beginning' and 'the father of it,' lying. Their true father. Hatred and lies betray whose children they are.
Because I tell the truth, ye believe not; 'he that is of God heareth God's words.' Deaf to truth. The unbelieving cannot hear the word of God.
Because I tell the truth, ye believe not; 'he that is of God heareth God's words.' Deaf to truth. The unbelieving cannot hear the word of God.
Because I tell the truth, ye believe not; 'he that is of God heareth God's words.' Deaf to truth. The unbelieving cannot hear the word of God.
'If a man keep my saying, he shall never see death'; thou hast a devil, they say. Promise scorned. They mock the very life He offers.
'If a man keep my saying, he shall never see death'; thou hast a devil, they say. Promise scorned. They mock the very life He offers.
'If a man keep my saying, he shall never see death'; thou hast a devil, they say. Promise scorned. They mock the very life He offers.
'If a man keep my saying, he shall never see death'; thou hast a devil, they say. Promise scorned. They mock the very life He offers.
Art thou greater than Abraham? 'if I honour myself, my honour is nothing'; the Father honours Me. Honoured by God. He seeks not His own glory but the Father's.
Art thou greater than Abraham? 'if I honour myself, my honour is nothing'; the Father honours Me. Honoured by God. He seeks not His own glory but the Father's.
Art thou greater than Abraham? 'if I honour myself, my honour is nothing'; the Father honours Me. Honoured by God. He seeks not His own glory but the Father's.
Art thou greater than Abraham? 'if I honour myself, my honour is nothing'; the Father honours Me. Honoured by God. He seeks not His own glory but the Father's.
'Before Abraham was, I am'; they take up stones, but He hides Himself. The eternal I AM. Christ claims the very name of God.
'Before Abraham was, I am'; they take up stones, but He hides Himself. The eternal I AM. Christ claims the very name of God.
